name: Bold Street Time Slips
description: The "Bold Street Time Slips" refer to a series of anecdotal accounts primarily centered around Bold Street in Liverpool, England, where individuals claim to have experienced brief, unintentional shifts in time. Proponents suggest that pedestrians have momentarily found themselves in a different era, witnessing clothing styles, vehicles, and shop fronts that do not belong to the present day. These experiences are typically short-lived, lasting only a few seconds or minutes, before the 'time slip' ends and the individual returns to their present reality. The purported origins of these claims can be traced back to the late 20th century, with a significant increase in reported incidents following the 1990s. Key evidence cited by proponents includes eyewitness accounts, personal testimonies shared online and in paranormal forums, and alleged photographic evidence, often blurry or circumstantial. Mainstream scholarly perspective dismisses these claims as misinterpretations of real-world events, psychological phenomena such as déjà vu, pareidolia (seeing patterns in random stimuli), or confabulation (unintentional false memories). Skeptics also point to the lack of verifiable physical evidence and the subjective nature of eyewitness testimony. Furthermore, the power of suggestion and the influence of local folklore could contribute to the perception of time slips in a location already imbued with historical significance. The architecture and historical context of Bold Street, with its mix of old and new buildings, could also contribute to feelings of displacement or disorientation, leading to misinterpretations of sensory input.
subtitle: Glimpses into Liverpool's past?

claims.claim: People momentarily experience a different time period on Bold Street.
claims.evidence: Eyewitness accounts of seeing different clothing, vehicles, and shopfronts.
claims.criticism: Eyewitness testimony is unreliable and prone to suggestion, misinterpretation, and memory distortion. Lack of objective evidence.

claims.claim: Bold Street is a 'hotspot' for paranormal activity, specifically time slips.
claims.evidence: Frequency of reported incidents concentrated on this location.
claims.criticism: The concentration of reports may be due to the street's high foot traffic and the pre-existing belief in time slips, leading to confirmation bias.

claims.claim: Photographic or video evidence captures anomalies indicative of a time slip.
claims.evidence: Blurry photos or videos purportedly showing anachronistic elements.
claims.criticism: Such evidence is often low quality, easily explained by natural phenomena, or potentially fabricated. Pareidolia and confirmation bias play a role.

keyFigures.name: Tom Slemen
keyFigures.role: Liverpool-based author who popularized many paranormal tales, including Bold Street Time Slips.

keyFigures.name: Deborah Hyde
keyFigures.role: Skeptic and editor of The Skeptic magazine, who has critiqued paranormal claims including time slips.

timeline.event: Initial reports of time slips on Bold Street begin to surface.
Increased reporting and popularization of the phenomenon through books and online forums.

sources.author: Peter Hough and Jenny Randles
sources.work: The Afterlife: An Investigation Into the Mysteries of Life After Death
sources.title: Peter Hough and Jenny Randles

sources.author: Richard Wiseman
sources.work: Paranormality: Why We See What Isn't There
sources.title: Richard Wiseman
